conventions: Loaded repo guidance from README.md, CLAUDE.md, apps/backend/README.md, apps/backend/pyproject.toml, active OpenSpec change deepen-airflow-state-seam, relevant OpenSpec backend specs, and the code-audit Python language pack. Backend conventions: FastAPI + uv on Python 3.13, httpx async Airflow client, pydantic-settings Config injected at startup, strict mypy on src, ruff with no print, module-level loggers, dependency flow main -> api -> core -> {models, utils}, frontend never calls Airflow directly, credentials only from env/.env.
summary: Deep-reviewed the Airflow status backend package and its tests in paths mode. The new Airflow state resolver preserves the documented v1 call budget and ordering, skips the freshness-state batch call for empty recent runs, and propagates client exceptions back to snapshot-builder isolation. The surrounding cache, API, config, registry, freshness, status, and Airflow client code match the loaded repo/spec conventions closely enough that no actionable findings were confirmed.

coverage.notes: Read every tracked Python file under apps/backend/src/airflow_status and apps/backend/tests completely. Also read directly relevant repo guidance, pyproject configuration, active OpenSpec proposal/design/tasks/spec deltas, base backend OpenSpec specs, and local Airflow 2.x endpoint/schema source for the taskInstances/list payload question.

what_went_well: The new core/airflow_state.py seam localizes Airflow call choreography and keeps snapshot_builder focused on projection, failure isolation, and bounded concurrency.
AirflowClient applies bounded timeouts, retries only transport/timeouts/5xx, uses timezone-aware UTC parsing, and avoids per-run HTTP calls for freshness states.

Tests cover resolver call ordering, skip-on-empty behavior, exception propagation, snapshot failure isolation, cache single-flight behavior, config validation, registry validation, freshness formatting, status precedence, and API envelopes.
notes_limitations: No tests, lint, or typecheck commands were run because the user constrained writes to the JSON artifact only; pytest/ruff/mypy may create cache artifacts in the workspace.
The working tree already had unrelated graphify-out modifications before the artifact write; they were not inspected as part of the requested paths and were left untouched.
Airflow API payload behavior was checked against local installed Airflow 2.x source plus official documentation search results; no live Airflow 2.9.1 instance was exercised.
Dependency vulnerability scanning, production deployment hardening, authN/authZ threat modeling, and frontend behavior were outside this paths-mode audit.
